lens: REGRESSION + NEW-RACE HUNT on CONC v3 (memory model CONC-0/MM-0..10 + the three repaired mechanisms). Walk each prior FATAL through v3; then attack every v3 fix as new fact-channel/authority surface (capture-loan machinery CONC-V3-3/4, Shareable gfp CONC-V3-9, guard_uniq clause CONC-V3-13/AMD-5). A hole = a data race, which D1 declares impossible.

regression.F-race-1_parent-write-and-two-spawn-uniq: CLOSED. Parent `set a` under a live shr capture entry rejects at R6 first sentence (assigned-over) / OWN-5; a second `&uniq 'p a` capture rejects at R5 (uniq issue overlapping the live uniq entry on the scope holder). The persistent scope-holder entries (CONC-V3-3) genuinely survive across statements, so the two DIFFERENT spawn statements are now compared (uniq+uniq rejects at R5; uniq+shr rejects at R5; shr+shr coexists). Verified sound.
regression.F-race-2_container-endpoint-clone: CLOSED (two independent bars). seq<cq_tx<'qa,u64>,4> is Shareable iff cq_tx is Shareable (CONC-V3-9 seq row) and cq_tx is never Shareable (CQ-3 row) => not Shareable => the replicate-& capture fails the AMD-7 premise; independently the cq_tx_clone &uniq re-mode (CONC-V3-11) makes a concurrent clone unspellable. Verified sound.
regression.F-race-3_guard-interior-view-escape: NOT CLOSED -> see FATAL finding. The two-view and early-drop variants close (R6 freeze of g / R7 overlapped-drop), but the escape-past-unlock variant reopens: guard_uniq's result region 'v is spelled as a caller-supplied region parameter (['x,'v], OWN-3), not locally-introduced, so the stated R13/GIVE-1/OWN-4 defense does not fire.
regression.F-race-return_out-of-scope-UAF: CLOSED for break/return/fallthrough (CONC-V3-4 joins before the leaving edge's drops), with a MINOR gap: the try-statement Err-propagation edge is not in the enumeration.
regression.cross-function-recursive-lock: SOUND but mislabeled: MM-3 O1 is a runtime block/trap, not the static 'REJECT' the regression appendix claims; no race (liveness failure), see NOTE.

findings.surface: CONC-3 guard_uniq form-table row (KERNEL-DELTAS-DRAFT.md line 316) + CONC-V3-13 (lines 329-337) + F-race-3 regression claim (lines 427-431), vs OWN-3/OWN-4, GIVE-1, R13, and the arena_get precedent (line 1028 / BRAND-1.3 line 776)
findings.title: guard_uniq's result region 'v is a CALLER-SUPPLIED region parameter, not locally-introduced, so the caller books the interior view at an outer region and gives/returns it past the unlock — the third FATAL (lock-free &uniq racing a locked writer) reopens at the exact clause v3 names as the fix
findings.program_or_defect: The row is `guard_uniq | ['x, 'v](g: &'x guard<'m, T>) -> &uniq 'v T | ISSUE uniq on source(guard-holder), result region 'v (dedicated)`. Here 'v sits in the region-param list ['x, 'v]; OWN-3 states verbatim that region_params 'introduce caller-supplied regions'. So the CALLER chooses 'v. CONC-V3-13's escape defense is 'because the view is at the locally-introduced 'v, it can never be returned or given past the guard (R13/GIVE-1/OWN-4 reject a borrow at a locally-introduced region)' — but a caller-supplied region param is NOT locally-introduced, so R13/GIVE-1/OWN-4 do NOT fire. Concrete reopen: `region 'o { let keep: &uniq 'o u64 = match sel { True() => { region 'i { let mr: &'i mutex<u64> = &'i shared_mx; let g: own guard<'i, u64> = mtx_lock(mr); let v: &uniq 'o u64 = guard_uniq<'i, 'o>(g); give v; } } False() => { ... } } set deref(keep) = 999_u64; }`. The give of v is judged as a returned borrow (GIVE-1); OWN-4 asks only 'o outlives-or-equals 'o (the destination keep is at 'o) — reflexively TRUE — so the give ACCEPTS. At `region 'i` end g auto-consumes (unlock); the draft never states the (g,uniq,view) entry is an R8-transferable holder that follows v out to 'o, and its own defense (locally-introduced 'v) is void, so under the natural reading the view entry expires with v's inner binding, g's R7 overlapped-drop check sees no live entry, the unlock succeeds, and `set deref(keep)` writes the now-unlocked interior lock-free while a sibling child holding shared_mx (a Shareable mutex<u64> captured &'p into it, striped-lock ACCEPT) writes under the lock: two threads, one location, one unsynchronized. This is precisely the mistake arena_get was repaired for — BRAND-1.3 says 'there is NO free result region 'v — a caller could otherwise book a content borrow at a region outliving the arena, an accepted UAF' and returns at the arena's OWN region 'a carried by the `ar` parameter. guard_uniq cites 'the pool_entry pattern' but does the opposite: it introduces a free 'v.
findings.minimal_fix: Delete the free result-region parameter. Mirror arena_get exactly: return the interior at a region CARRIED BY the guard parameter and bounded by the guard's life — either 'x (the `&'x g` receiver region) or 'm — so OWN-4/R13 genuinely reject any give/return past the guard (there is no caller-chosen region to book against). If a fresh nested view region is truly wanted, it needs a stated compiler-fresh-region mechanism (none exists in the kernel; a region_param is caller-supplied by OWN-3), AND the (g,uniq,view) entry must be specified as an R8-transferable holder so the R7 overlapped-drop check is a real backstop. Re-review the AMD-5 carve-out after: its interior-exclusivity soundness reduces entirely to the view not outliving g's tenure, which only the region-tie provides.

findings.surface: CONC-1 scope effect row, CONC-V3-6 (lines 185-189) 'the UNION of each body's row instantiated at the actual capture regions, dropping ONLY regions introduced inside the scope or a body', vs EFF-2 both-ways and OWN-12
findings.title: 'Instantiated at the actual capture regions' under the natural reading uses the capture's scope-local annotation region 'p (which the clause then DROPS), re-hiding a child's writes to a caller region — the exact cross-thread write channel CONC-V3-6 claims to close
findings.program_or_defect: A body is a named fn, e.g. `fn writer['w](x: &uniq 'w u64) -> own unit writes('w) { set deref(x) = 7_u64; }` — it exhibits writes('w), its own parameter region, and knows nothing of any caller region. Consider `fn hidden_write['b](xb: &uniq 'b u64) -> own unit writes('b) { scope 'p { let s = spawn['p](writer, x: &uniq 'p deref(xb)); } return unit; }`. The capture `&uniq 'p deref(xb)` is legal (OWN-10: 'b outlives 'p). At the spawn, writer's 'w instantiates to the capture's stated region 'p, so the body row is writes('p). CONC-V3-6 drops regions 'introduced inside the scope' — 'p is the scope-introduced region — so the union row drops writes('p). Result: hidden_write EXHIBITS no write to 'b, even though the child wrote caller storage at 'b through xb. EFF-2 checks both ways: the honest declaration writes('b) is now 'declared-but-unexhibited' and is REJECTED, forcing the author to drop it, after which hidden_write ships claiming purity/no-'b-write while it does write 'b — a false optimizer fact (a caller may treat 'b's target as unchanged across the call). Not a data race (the scope joins before hidden_write returns and the caller is frozen out of 'b for the whole call by OWN-5), but a false effect fact, which is optimizer-unsound in this project. The fix's correctness hinges on 'the actual capture regions' meaning the ROOT region of the captured place ('b), not the annotation region 'p; as worded and under the natural implementation (instantiate the callee region param with the capture argument's stated region) it means 'p and the hole persists.
findings.minimal_fix: Pin the wording: the scope row instantiates each body row at the ROOT region of each captured place (the region the captured borrow is rooted at per OWN-10), and drops a region only if that ROOT region is introduced inside the scope/body. Equivalently, forbid capturing a caller-rooted place at a scope-local region (require `&uniq 'b deref(xb)`, at the root region), so writes('b) is exhibited and never dropped. State it with the caller-rooted example from the clause so the checker cannot read 'capture region' as the 'p annotation.

findings.surface: CONC-V3-4 (lines 163-170) scope-exit edge enumeration '(fallthrough, break to an outer label, and return), matching STOR-3' vs GRAM-4 try_stmt and ERR-3
findings.title: The scope-exit enumeration omits the try-statement Err-propagation edge; read as exhaustive it lets a `try` inside a scope pop the frame under a live child (UAF) without joining
findings.program_or_defect: GRAM-4 lists try_stmt as a distinct statement kind from return_stmt/break_stmt. `let x: own T = try e;` on the Err arm returns from the function (ERR-3), which IS an edge leaving the scope block, but it is neither syntactically a `return` nor a `break`. CONC-V3-4's general clause 'every control-flow edge leaving the scope block' does cover it, but the parenthetical '(fallthrough, break to an outer label, and return), matching STOR-3's edge enumeration' — and STOR-3 itself — enumerate only those three by name (both rely on try elaborating to return). A checker that implements the enumeration by matching statement kinds (return/break/fallthrough) misses try_stmt's Err edge; then `fn f['?]() -> own Result<unit,E> { let a: own u64 = 0_u64; scope 'p { let sp = spawn['p](writer, x: &uniq 'p a); let r: own T = try g(); ... } }` pops `a`'s frame on the Err path WITHOUT joining the child holding `&uniq 'p a` -> UAF/race. Spec-level it is covered by elaboration, but the delta invites the misread by giving a specific (incomplete) enumeration for a fix whose whole point is edge-completeness.
findings.minimal_fix: Add the try-propagation edge explicitly to CONC-V3-4's enumeration: 'every control-flow edge leaving the scope block — fallthrough, break to an outer label, return, AND a try-statement Err propagation — joins all children first, before that edge's R7/STOR-3 releases and drops.'

findings.surface: CONC-V3-3 (lines 160-161) 'own Sendable captures are moved into the child and consumed at the spawn' + CONC-V3-2 SpawnError/Eagain ('returned before the child ever runs')
findings.title: An `own` capture is consumed at the spawn statement, but on the Err(Eagain) path the child never runs — the moved-in value's fate (leak, or a drop that may double-free / run user drop code off-thread) is unspecified
findings.program_or_defect: `let s1: own Result<unit, SpawnError> = spawn['p](worker, buf: move b);` moves b into the child env at the spawn statement (the parent binding b is dead by OWN-1 regardless of the runtime result). CONC-V3-2 says Eagain 'is returned before the child ever runs'. If the child never runs, the moved-in b (say a `box<T>`, affine, with a compiler-derived drop) is in limbo: the parent already consumed it, and no child will drop it. The draft does not state who drops it on the Eagain arm. If nobody does, it leaks; if the spawn runtime drops it, that is a compiler-derived drop of a value on a thread boundary with no stated ordering (MM-1's publication edge to the child body is vacuous when the body never runs), and for a value whose drop runs user-visible code this is an off-contract execution — an edge that borders D1 (a mis-specified double-free) rather than plainly outside it.
findings.minimal_fix: Specify the Eagain contract: an `own` capture is moved into the child ONLY on the Ok path; on Err(Eagain) the value is either handed back in the error or dropped by the spawning thread before spawn returns (a normal-path, trap-free, same-thread drop), stated as part of spawn's form contract and the MM-1 edge.

findings.surface: CONC-V3-9 Sendable/Shareable table (lines 237-248): the 'user struct'/'user enum' rows vs the 'any confined(...) type' row + CONC-V3-10 (lines 258-262)
findings.title: A confined user struct matches both the 'user struct: iff every field' row and the 'confined: never' row; the table states no precedence, so the fail-closed 'never' is guaranteed only by prose, not by the normative table
findings.program_or_defect: R1 permits a `confined(shr)` user struct whose fields are all owned non-confined Sendable types (it need not carry a borrow field; the region param is required only 'if it can hold a loan'). Such a struct matches the table's 'user struct' row (Sendable iff every field Sendable => could be YES) AND the 'any confined(...) type (form-table token OR user)' row (never). The table presents these as parallel rows with no stated ordering. CONC-V3-10 prose resolves it ('Confined = never (repartition by R1)'), but the normative table alone is ambiguous, and a checker keyed off the structural row first would compute a wrong YES for a degenerate all-owned confined struct — exactly the 'filed a user confined struct as ordinary owned' error CONC-V3-10 says v2 made.
findings.minimal_fix: State precedence in the table/normative text: the confined-type classification is checked FIRST and dominates; the 'user struct'/'user enum' rows read 'NON-confined user struct/enum'. One clause, no new mechanism.

findings.surface: CONC-V3-16 regression appendix, 'Cross-function re-lock REJECT ... under MM-3 O1' (lines 438-440) vs MM-3 O1 (lines 66-70) as written
findings.title: MM-3 O1 is a runtime block/trap obligation on the implementation, not a static rejection; labeling the cross-function re-lock a 'REJECT' conflates the two and overstates what the checker does
findings.program_or_defect: MM-3 O1 says a same-instance re-acquire 'must BLOCK or TRAP, never issue a second token' — a runtime guarantee. The cross-function program `let g1 = mtx_lock(&'m m); helper(&'m m);` (helper re-locks) is NOT statically rejected: helper's fresh per-function loan table (R10c) has no entry on m's interior, so mtx_lock is statically callable; at runtime the second acquire blocks (deadlock) or traps. The outcome is sound (no race — the second tenure never issues, so no second live &uniq interior view is ever produced; the static two-views point is unreachable and the two views are never used across the alias within one frame), but it is a liveness failure, not a static 'REJECT'. Calling it a REJECT in the regression appendix mis-describes the mechanism and could mislead the model phase into expecting a compile-time diagnostic.
findings.minimal_fix: Reword the appendix line to 'runtime-blocks (deadlock) or traps under MM-3 O1 — a liveness failure outside D1, not a static rejection', matching the prior review's own characterization, and keep O1 as an implementation proof obligation in the CONC-0 list.

verdict: NOT ADOPTABLE (one FATAL remains). v3 genuinely closes two of the three prior FATALs: the spawn-capture race is closed by the persistent scope-holder loan entries (CONC-V3-3) + R5/R6 now comparing across statements (parent-write, two-spawn uniq overlap, and uniq-vs-shr in DIFFERENT spawn statements all reject), and the container-endpoint-clone race is closed twice over (the seq/cq_tx compositional Shareable rule + AMD-7, and the cq_tx_clone &uniq re-mode). The Shareable greatest-fixpoint is sound (assume-true-on-cycle never masks a non-Sendable leaf, because slice/ahdl/confined cannot occupy the recursive/field positions and the mutex row correctly consults Sendable(interior); fail-closed catches every unlisted type), and the return/break out-of-scope UAF is closed by CONC-V3-4's join-before-drop. But FATAL-3 (guard interior-view escape) is NOT closed: guard_uniq's result region 'v is spelled as a caller-supplied region parameter (['x, 'v], OWN-3), so the repair's own escape defense — 'a borrow at a locally-introduced region cannot be returned/given' — is void, and the caller can book the view at an outer region and give/return it past the unlock, reopening the lock-free-&uniq-vs-locked-writer data race that D1 declares impossible. This is the same free-result-region mistake arena_get was explicitly repaired for, which guard_uniq re-commits while citing arena_get's pattern. Beneath it, a MAJOR effect-row hole: CONC-V3-6's 'instantiated at the actual capture regions' under the natural reading uses the scope-local annotation region (then drops it), re-hiding a child's write to a caller region — the exact channel the clause claims to close — a false optimizer fact. Plus three MINORs (the try Err-edge missing from the CONC-V3-4 enumeration; the own-capture fate on Eagain; confined-vs-user-struct table precedence) and one NOTE (MM-3 O1 mislabeled 'REJECT'). Per the standing rule each of these is new fact-channel/authority surface: fix the guard_uniq result region (tie it to the guard's own parameter-carried region, no free 'v; specify the view entry as an R8-transferable holder), pin the effect-row instantiation to the captured place's root region, then RE-ATTACK the repaired text before any adoption.
hardest_failed: Shareable gfp wrong-TRUE by hiding a non-Sendable leaf behind a mutex inside a recursion cycle — tried struct T{m:mutex<U>} with struct U{x:cq_rx<'q,u64>, back:box<T>} and variants routing slice/ahdl/confined through the cycle. DEFEATED: non-Sendable leaves cannot occupy the recursive positions (structs carry no region parameter so no slice/ahdl field; R1 bars confined fields in non-confined types; BRAND-1.3/B1V22-5 bars ahdl outside box/Option), and the mutex row correctly evaluates Sendable(interior) not Shareable(interior); every 'false' therefore originates at a non-cyclic leaf and propagates, so assume-true-on-cycle never masks it. gfp terminates and gives the right answer.

Early-drop of a capture loan to let the parent regain access mid-child-life — via an inner `scope 'q`, a `match` arm that returns, a `break @l` to an inner loop label, and a nested-region exit. DEFEATED: the scope holder is one-per-scope and compiler-introduced (no user consume), and only an edge leaving 'p's own block removes 'p's entries; inner scopes/loops/matches leave them live, and R12/AMD-4 excludes the diverging arm from agreement, so no leaving-edge short of 'p's exit frees a captured place.

mutex<cq_tx<'q,u64>> (spsc) shared &'p across a scope, many threads serially locking and sending on the one producer — attacked as an spsc invariant break (spsc assumes exactly one producer thread; even plain non-atomic head writes). DEFEATED / soundly ACCEPTS: MM-3 O2 makes unlock_i synchronize-with lock-return_{i+1}, so each tenure's producer-cursor writes (even plain) happen-before the next tenure's reads; sound provided the impl keeps its cursor cache in the endpoint struct, not TLS — which MM-5 mandates ('never thread identity'). A real obligation for the model phase, but not a hole in the rules.

Loop-spawn into an OUTER scope 'p capturing a loop-body-local region (`spawn['p](worker, x: &uniq 'r b)` with 'r,b loop-local) so a 'p-joined child outlives 'r. DEFEATED twice: R12 back-edge equality rejects (each iteration adds a capture entry to holder_p, so the back-edge state != entry state), and independently R7's overlapped-drop check fires when b is dropped at iteration end under the live (b,uniq,holder_p) entry.

Second interior view of one guard bypassing the g-freeze — two guard_uniq calls, and a `&uniq 'y g` mint alongside a view. DEFEATED: the first (g,uniq,view) entry freezes g (R6: while a uniq entry on P is live, no operation on or borrow mint from P is permitted), so the second guard_uniq's `&'x2 g` receiver mint and any other borrow of g reject. The single-guard exclusivity holds; only the ESCAPE of the one legitimate view (the FATAL) breaks it.

lens: THE MEMORY MODEL FOUNDATION (CONC-0 / MM-0..MM-10) and the R15/AMD-8 obligation: whether MM-0's data-race definition is a non-circular reduction target the sealed-form proofs discharge to (walked on the SPSC queue via MM-4/MM-5); whether MM-3 O1-O4 outlaws the cross-function recursive re-lock and composes with the MM-1/MM-2 spawn/join edges; whether the MM-7 `sync` effect atom is well-defined against EFF-1/EFF-2 and its R14 replicated-slot exception hole-free; whether AMD-8 behavior-based attachment is well-defined and reaches the loan-NONE clone rows; and whether MM-9/MM-10 plus the CONC-4 fault-injection battery close the trap-abort + in-flight-io story.

regression.F-race-1 (two-spawn &uniq overlap / spaw: REJECTS, sound. CONC-V3-3's persistent (a, uniq|shr, scope-holder) capture entry is live to scope exit; the second &uniq capture is an R5 uniq-overlap reject, the parent `set a` an R6 freeze reject. The statement-local AMD-1 evaporation the prior FATAL exploited is genuinely repaired by lifting the entry to scope-holder life. Field-level overlap (a.f vs a) is handled correctly by R4 prefix-overlap.
regression.F-race-2 (container-Shareable endpoint-c: REJECTS twice, sound. seq<cq_tx> is not Shareable under the CONC-V3-9 structural induction (Shareable(cq_tx)=never per CQ-3), so the replicate-& capture fails the AMD-7 premise; independently the CONC-V3-11 &uniq re-mode makes a concurrent clone unspellable (seq_get through &'p yields &, cq_tx_clone now needs &uniq). Greatest-fixpoint assume-on-cycle is sound (non-Sendability/Shareability originate only at leaves, never at a box-cycle).
regression.F-race-3 (guard interior-view escape): REJECTS, sound modulo one under-specified mechanism. guard_uniq returns &uniq at a dedicated locally-introduced 'v so return/give fail R13/GIVE-1/OWN-4; the (g, uniq, view) entry freezes g so a second view fails R5 and an early unlock fails R7. RESIDUAL: the entry is held by a plain &uniq borrow, but R4/R7 holders and R10(b) issuance are defined only for CONFINED results; the borrow-held-entry lifetime (tie to 'v, removal at 'v end) is new machinery that CONC-V3-13 flags as a marked AMD-5 carve-out but does not fully specify. Under-specification here can only over-reject (leak/over-freeze), not race.
regression.cross-function recursive re-lock (prior: REJECTS-as-safe. MM-3 O1 forces block-or-trap on a same-instance re-acquire, so no second token issues; outcome is self-deadlock or abort, never two live guards. Liveness failure (CAP-1 out of scope), not a race.
regression.prior MAJORs (publication basis; mtx eff: ADDRESSED. MM-1/MM-2 (CONC-V3-5) give spawn/join publication a normative edge; mtx_lock is rowed `sync` (MM-7), dissolving the writes('m)-rejects-Walk3A / pure-invites-dedup / reads('m)-is-a-lie trilemma the prior review posed.
regression.overall: All 3 prior FATALs and both prior MAJORs are structurally repaired; no new checker-accepted data race is spellable. Residual defects are model-precision (MAJOR x2) and spec-consistency (MINOR x4), not accepted races.

findings.surface: CONC-0 MM-0 (line 48-50, HB defined as "program order ... plus the closed synchronizes-with edge list MM-1..MM-6") composed with MM-4 (lines 74-77) and the CONC-0 preamble (lines 42-44, "a sealed author proves 'my internals establish these synchronizes-with edges'")
findings.title: MM-1..MM-6 are stated as UNCONDITIONAL definitional HB edges while ALSO being the per-form proof obligations, so the reduction target is vacuously dischargeable; MM-4 additionally lacks the release/acquire memory-event granularity, reads-from, and cumulative-visibility content the SPSC payload proof actually needs
findings.program_or_defect: MM-0 folds MM-1..MM-6 into the DEFINITION of HB, and MM-4 asserts "cq_send(v) synchronizes-with the cq_recv returning that v" as a flat fact at OPERATION granularity. But the preamble says the author must PROVE the edges. These two roles are conflated: as literally written, HB CONTAINS the send->recv edge unconditionally, so a sealed queue whose cq_send emits a RELAXED (non-release) store still satisfies MM-4's definitional edge and is "proven" race-free by MM-0 -- while a real arm64 execution races the payload. Walk the SPSC with a box payload: producer `write *box; cq_send(tx, box)`, consumer `let box2 = cq_recv(rx); read *box2`. The conflicting language-managed accesses are `write *box` and `read *box2` on the same heap allocation, distinct threads. The reduction that makes them HB-ordered needs THREE facts MM-4 does not state: (i) which internal memory event is the release store and which is the acquire load; (ii) reads-from -- recv's acquire actually reads the value send's release wrote (or a later one in its release sequence), not some other send's; (iii) cumulative visibility -- everything po-before cq_send (including the user's `write *box`) is HB-before everything po-after the matching cq_recv. MM-0's "program order within one thread" is also not defined to range over sealed-op-internal memory events, yet the chain write->(po)->release-store requires exactly that. Without (i)-(iii) and the conditional framing, the sealed author can 'discharge' MM-4 by citing the definitional edge -- the exact vacuity CONC-0 was built to remove. This is not circular in INTENT (the per-form list at lines 126-127 assigns the discharge) but is circular AS WRITTEN.
findings.minimal_fix: State the theorem CONDITIONALLY: "IF every sealed form discharges its MM-1..MM-6 edges via its release/acquire fences (MM-10 lowering), THEN HB contains those edges and an accepted program has no data race." Restate MM-4 at memory-event granularity -- name the publishing release store and the consuming acquire load, require reads-from, and state cumulative visibility (everything po-before cq_send is HB-before everything po-after the matching cq_recv, including user writes through a moved-in box). Define MM-0 program-order to include sealed-op-internal memory events. Keep MM-1/2/3/6 the same two-role split.

findings.surface: CONC-0 MM-9 (lines 111-114) and CONC-4 point 4 (lines 370-374): "external ... state receives power-loss-prefix semantics"
findings.title: "power-loss-prefix semantics" is an overclaim under io_uring's out-of-order async completion: in-flight writes submitted before the trap can land after the abort out of order, giving a HOLE not a prefix, breaking the named WAL-recovery consumer
findings.program_or_defect: MM-9 closes the PROCESS-MEMORY half correctly (whole-process abort leaves no surviving in-process observer, the sticky-flag handler is the only writer and is TCB, so skipping teardown is D1-sound). But it hands the external half to the fault-injection battery under the label "power-loss-prefix semantics," which is false for the pinned io_uring target. Concrete: a KV WAL submits writes W1, W2, W3 to the log via io_uring SQEs without IOSQE_IO_LINK/drain, then a sibling thread traps. The kernel owns the already-submitted SQEs and may execute them concurrently/out-of-order after the process dies, landing W1 and W3 but NOT W2 -- a hole in the middle, not a prefix. A recovery path that assumes a torn TAIL only (prefix W1..Wk) reads a corrupt record and mis-recovers. This is outside D1 (external/persisted state, excluded by SCOPE-3's language-managed scoping) so it is not a data race, but the model states a durability guarantee it has not earned and a named consumer relies on it.
findings.minimal_fix: Replace "power-loss-prefix semantics" with the honest io_uring guarantee: an arbitrary SUBSET of writes submitted-before-trap may land after abort, possibly out of order; a durability point requires an explicit submission barrier (IO_LINK chain, drain, or fsync) before it, and only writes preceding a COMPLETED barrier are guaranteed. Require the CONC-4 fault-injection battery to exercise OUT-OF-ORDER completion (drop a middle write, land a later one), not only torn-tail, and re-scope the WAL consumer to the subset+barrier model.

findings.surface: CONC-0 per-form obligation list (lines 120-122) and AMD-8 (lines 299-308): "attached by BEHAVIOR ('the body may access state reachable from another live binding or thread') not by loan class"
findings.title: AMD-8 "behavior-based attachment" is a misnomer: sealed-op bodies are TCB-opaque so the checker cannot compute 'accesses cross-thread-reachable state'; attachment is really the syntactic `sync` marker plus a hand-curated enumeration whose completeness is an unverifiable trust obligation
findings.program_or_defect: The draft frames attachment as a BEHAVIOR judgment on the op's body, but immediately supplies an explicit enumeration (mtx_new, mtx_lock, guard_uniq, guard drop, all ten cq_*, both clone rows, endpoint drops, cq_new). The checker has no access to the sealed body (TCB, SCOPE-3), so it cannot verify 'this op accesses another-thread-reachable state' -- that is a declaration made by the form-table author. What the checker CAN attach on is the `sync` atom in the effect row (MM-7). So the real, checkable rule is "in the AMD-8 net iff the row carries `sync`," plus an audited enumeration for completeness. Calling it 'by behavior' overstates mechanization and reintroduces exactly the judgment-call the prior review warned against. It DOES reach the loan-NONE clone rows (they are in the list and carry `sync`), so the coverage goal is met -- but the completeness of the list against opaque bodies is a trusted-review obligation, not a computed property.
findings.minimal_fix: Rename the attachment to what the checker does: "an op is in the AMD-8 obligation net iff its form-table row declares `sync`; the model-review author must ensure every op touching cross-thread-reachable state declares `sync` (unverifiable from the TCB body -- a trusted-declaration obligation), and the CONC-0 enumeration is the audited completeness check." Drop 'by BEHAVIOR' as if checker-computed.

findings.surface: CONC-0 MM-7 sync-carrying set (lines 95-96) vs the mutex form-table rows (lines 314, 316) vs the CONC-0 obligation list (line 124)
findings.title: Three non-identical enumerations of the sync/obligation ops contradict each other on the constructors and on guard_uniq, so "carries sync" is not equivalent to "in the AMD-8 net"
findings.program_or_defect: MM-7 says "every sealed sync op (mtx_lock, guard_uniq, the guard/endpoint drops, and every cq_* op) carries sync." But (a) the form-table rows guard_uniq is `reads('x)`, NOT `sync` (line 316) -- direct contradiction with MM-7's list; (b) mtx_new is `pure` (line 314) yet appears in the CONC-0 obligation list (line 124); (c) "every cq_* op" would force `sync` onto cq_new, which does no cross-thread publication at mint (both endpoints still local), matching mtx_new's pure-constructor status. So the sync marker (MM-7), the form rows, and the obligation list disagree, defeating the clean 'carries sync <=> in the AMD-8 net' equivalence that finding-3's fix depends on. Note guard_uniq likely does NOT need `sync` -- its interior-view write is already fenced by mtx_lock's acquire (`sync` is po-before it and MM-7 forbids reordering across the acquire direction), so `reads('x)` suffices; MM-7's list is over-inclusive there.
findings.minimal_fix: Make "carries `sync`" exactly "in the AMD-8 net." Recommended: drop mtx_new/cq_new from the sync set and the obligation net (pure constructors, no publication -- review them once as vacuous and move on) and drop guard_uniq from MM-7's sync list (keep `reads('x)`; its write is fenced by mtx_lock). Otherwise add `sync` to those rows explicitly. Reconcile MM-7, the form rows, and line 124 to one list.

findings.surface: CONC-0 MM-7 (line 95, "a new EFF-1 effect atom `sync`") vs kernel-spec EFF-1 (line 285) and EFF-2 (line 287)
findings.title: MM-7 adds `sync` to the effect grammar but does not amend EFF-1's CLOSED canonical order or state EFF-2's syntactic exhibits rule, leaving `sync`'s row position undefined for the R10(c)/R12 row-equality checks
findings.program_or_defect: EFF-1 fixes the effect row to "exactly this canonical order (reads, writes, allocates, traps)" with `pure` as the unique empty spelling. Introducing `sync` requires (i) extending the production, (ii) pinning `sync`'s position in the canonical order, and (iii) an EFF-2 exhibits clause defining when a declaration exhibits `sync` (checked both ways). Row-equality is load-bearing elsewhere: R10(c) requires "live entries ... equal exactly the signature-implied caller effect" and R12/AMD-4 compares states including rows; if `sync`'s canonical position is unpinned, a caller-declared `sync, reads('m)` and a callee-derived `reads('m), sync` can spuriously mismatch. EFF-2 as written derives writes only "per borrow modes they use"; since mtx_lock's lock-word write rides a sealed body (not a syntactic set/index-write through its `&'m` shared borrow), EFF-2 will not derive it -- so the row IS `sync` alone, which is fine, but only once EFF-2 is told a call to a sync-rowed op exhibits `sync`.
findings.minimal_fix: Amend EFF-1 to place `sync` in the canonical order (e.g. `sync, reads, writes, allocates, traps`) and extend the grammar. Add EFF-2: "a declaration exhibits `sync` iff its body or requires block calls any operation whose row includes `sync`; checked both ways." Confirm R10(c)/R12 normalize to the amended order. Land these in the same change as the MM-7 kernel addition (already on open-flag 1).

findings.surface: Walk 3A (line 465) "R14's no-writes clause refers to loan-judged source writes ... R15/AMD-8-certified sync internals are the reviewed exception" vs ratified R14 (RULES-RATIFIED line 41) and AMD-7 (lines 286-291)
findings.title: The R14 "no writes through replicated slots" sync-exemption is carried only as walk prose, not marked rule text, and its soundness rests on an unstated invariant that every `sync`-rowed physical write is data-race-free
findings.program_or_defect: R14 ratified checks "each body's effect row must match its slot modes (no writes through replicated slots)." The striped-lock ACCEPT (Walk 3A / seq<mutex<u64>,4>) has each body write the lock word through a replicate-shared `&'p` slot. The checker accepts because mtx_lock is rowed `sync` (not writes('p)), so R14's no-writes check sees no writes('p) and passes -- but WHY that is sound (a physical write through a replicate slot is not a race) is asserted only in Walk 3A prose. AMD-7 (the one marked R14 amendment) adds the capability premise, NOT this reinterpretation. The load-bearing invariant is "every `sync`-rowed physical write is data-race-free by the op's discharged O1-O4 exclusion (MM-3) or an atomic-RMW contract." I could not spell an abuse -- only Shareable interior-mutable forms reach a replicate-& slot (AMD-7), and today the only such form is the mutex family (O1-O4-gated); a plain `set q[i]` through the slot stays writes('p) by EFF-2 and R14 rejects it. But the exemption swallows R14's clause the instant a Shareable sync form appears whose write is NOT exclusion/atomic-backed, and nothing states that as the gating invariant.
findings.minimal_fix: Add a marked amendment (extend AMD-7 or a new AMD): "R14's no-writes-through-replicated-slots ranges over loan-judged source writes (`writes(...)` rows); `sync`-rowed ops are the reviewed exception." State the load-bearing invariant explicitly: every `sync`-rowed physical write must be data-race-free by discharged O1-O4 exclusion or an atomic-RMW contract (an AMD-8 per-form obligation), and the only Shareable interior-mutable sealed forms are exclusion- or atomic-based.

verdict: HOLDS against concrete data-race construction, but the CONC-0 foundation is NOT YET a sound reduction target as written. All three prior FATALs genuinely reject (F-race-1 by the CONC-V3-3 scope-holder capture entry; F-race-2 by the CONC-V3-9 structural Shareable induction + the CONC-V3-11 &uniq clone re-mode; F-race-3 by the dedicated-'v interior view + R13/GIVE-1/OWN-4), and I could spell no new checker-accepted two-thread unsynchronized access. CONC-0 IS the right minimal model and correctly scoped -- each of MM-0..MM-10 earns its place, and the improvement over v2 (which had no reduction target at all) is real. MM-3 O1 genuinely outlaws the cross-function recursive re-lock (block/trap, never a second token -> deadlock/abort, not a race), and it composes cleanly with the MM-1/MM-2 spawn/join edges: a child-acquired lock is ordered before the parent's post-join access by BOTH the MM-2 join edge and the MM-3-O2 tenure handoff, with O1 preventing overlap (worst case is a join-before-unlock deadlock, which is D1-safe). But two MAJOR foundation defects remain. (1) The answer to \"is it still circular\": structurally no, but AS WRITTEN yes -- MM-1..MM-6 are folded into the DEFINITION of HB (MM-0) and MM-4 asserts the send->recv edge unconditionally at operation granularity, so a sealed queue with a relaxed (non-release) publish still satisfies the definitional edge and is 'proven' race-free while its box payload races on arm64; the discharge obligation exists only in the deferred per-form list, and MM-4 omits the release/acquire event identity, reads-from, and cumulative-visibility content the SPSC proof needs. (2) MM-9 overclaims external durability: \"power-loss-prefix semantics\" is false under io_uring out-of-order async completion, breaking the WAL-recovery consumer (outside D1, hence MAJOR not FATAL). Four MINOR defects: AMD-8 \"behavior-based\" attachment is really the `sync` marker plus a hand-curated enumeration (the checker cannot read TCB bodies); the sync-carrying set, the form rows, and the obligation list disagree on the constructors and guard_uniq; `sync` is added without amending EFF-1's canonical order or EFF-2's exhibits rule; and the R14 replicated-slot sync-exemption is walk prose, not marked rule text, resting on an unstated \"sync writes are race-free\" invariant. None restructures the model; all are precision/consistency fixes the model-checking phase must land before per-form discharge. Obligations still owed by the later phase: per-form discharge of MM-3 O1-O4 (mutex), MM-4/MM-5 (all cq ops, with the memory-event granularity from fix 1), and MM-6 (endpoint-drop acq_rel counting, unique free, drain-sees-all-published); MM-10 per-target C11/LLVM lowering proofs with the arm64 Chase-Lev/Vyukov fence audit; the corrected out-of-order-aware fault-injection battery; the EFF-1/EFF-2 `sync` grammar landing; the marked R14 sync-exemption amendment with its gating invariant; and full specification of the new borrow-held loan entry that guard_uniq's (g, uniq, view) requires (R4/R7/R10(b) today issue and track entries only for confined results).
hardest_failed: R14 sync-exemption abuse (hardest): tried to route a genuinely racy user-memory write through a replicate-shared `&'p` slot by hiding it behind a `sync` row. Defeated -- only Shareable referents reach a replicate-& slot (AMD-7), the only Shareable interior-mutable form is the mutex family, and its sync write is the lock word under O1-O4 exclusion; a plain `set q[i]` through the slot stays writes('p) by EFF-2 and R14 rejects it. Even mutex<cq_tx> routes its send under mtx_lock. No non-exclusion sync write is spellable today -- but the soundness is an unstated invariant (finding 6), not a proven closure.

mutex<cq_tx<'q,u64>> serialized multi-thread send on ONE spsc producer (subtlest ACCEPT): tried to make two threads' sends on the single shared producer race. Defeated -- mtx_lock O1 admits one tenure at a time, O2 chains unlock_i synchronizes-with lock_{i+1}, so send_i HB send_{i+1}; the endpoint's cached producer state is published across threads by the mutex release/acquire. Sound ONLY under MM-5's HB-seriality/never-thread-identity obligation on the spsc implementation (a thread-local cursor would break it).

Child locks a parent-held mutex across the MM-2 join: `scope 'p { let g1 = mtx_lock(&'m m); spawn['p](locker, m: &'p m); }` -- the child's mtx_lock(m) cannot issue a second tenure (O1 blocks), so the two-live-guards aliased-&uniq race never forms; join-before-unlock (CONC-V3-4) instead produces a deadlock (parent's g1 unlocks only after the join that waits on the blocked child). Deadlock is a liveness failure (CAP-1 out of scope), not a D1 race.

Greatest-fixpoint Shareable/Sendable forge on a cyclic type: enum L { Nil(); Cons(tx: cq_tx<'q,u64>, next: box<L>); } -- tried to make assume-on-cycle falsely grant Shareable. Defeated -- Shareable(L)=Shareable(cq_tx)=never (leaf), and Sendable(L)=true is correct (a list of Sendable endpoints IS sendable); non-Sendability/Shareability originate only at leaves, never at a box-cycle, so the greatest-fixpoint is sound.

SPSC box-payload data race under the reduction: producer `write *box; cq_send` vs consumer `cq_recv; read *box2`. The chain write*box ->(po)-> send-release ->(sw, MM-4)-> recv-acquire ->(po)-> read*box2 is HB, so no race -- the reduction composes. Only 'failed' in that it exposed finding 1: it composes solely if MM-4's edge is read at memory-event granularity with reads-from and cumulative visibility, which the text does not state.
